message:
chore(beads): close bd-7v4oe with 12x stress validation; file bd-el0v8 for new flakes

bd-7v4oe (setjmp registry flake) confirmed fixed by 6ee22896:
zero longjmp_entrypoint_terminates failures across 12 stress
iterations with --test-threads=16.

bd-el0v8 captures three sibling test flakes that surfaced in the
same sweep — synchronous_gai_wrappers (3/12), tmpfile (1/12),
fmemopen_write (1/12). Different roots from bd-7v4oe; same
RAII-cleanup-guard pattern likely applies to fmemopen but the
gai/tmpfile variants need their own analysis.

files.patch:
@@ -523,6 +523,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-7smr","title":"bug: closure gate should not treat stale .beads as source","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T13:42:52.918118286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T14:05:46.500275585Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T14:05:46.500003144Z","close_reason":"Closure gate now defaults to matrix source to avoid stale beads; beads source opt-in via env","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7t6b","title":"syscall: mount_setattr invalid dirfd should not override NULL pathname EINVAL","description":"Host glibc still returns EINVAL for mount_setattr(-1, NULL, 0, NULL, 0), so pathname validation wins over the bad dirfd in this degenerate case. Add focused ABI coverage for that precedence rule.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:37:42.214693289Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T19:55:03.836123521Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T19:55:03.833885623Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7thd","title":"syscall: eventfd_write invalid fd should preserve EBADF even for forbidden all-ones payload","description":"Host glibc returns EBADF for eventfd_write(-1, UINT64_MAX), so the invalid-fd contract dominates the forbidden payload. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:37:58.438270320Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T01:10:22.456939420Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T01:10:22.454702537Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-7v4oe","title":"setjmp_abi tests::longjmp_entrypoint_terminates_with_enosys_payload_in_tests flakes under high parallelism","description":"## Repro

```bash
for i in {1..8}; do
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test \
    -p frankenlibc-abi --tests -- --test-threads=16 \
    | grep -E "FAILED|invalid size|signal:"
done
```

After ~3-4 iterations the unit test
`setjmp_abi::tests::longjmp_entrypoint_terminates_with_enosys_payload_in_tests`
asserts FAILED. Running with `--test-threads=1` does not reproduce.
Found while stress-testing the bd-wbqeo per-thread reentry refactor;
the failure is unrelated to that change (also reproduces against
prior commits — file history shows the test pattern is unchanged).

## Suspected cause

The setjmp/longjmp test uses a shared
`OnceLock<Mutex<HashMap<usize, JumpRegistryEntry>>>` keyed by env
pointer address (crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/setjmp_abi.rs:44). The
unit test creates a stack-local `marker: [u64; 16]` and uses its
address as the env_addr key. Two test functions running on different
worker threads get distinct stack addresses, but a single worker
thread that runs tests sequentially reuses the same stack frame
addresses across tests — the registry entry from a prior test
remains for that key and is overwritten by the next capture. The
new entry's bytes should match the new capture's mem_bytes, but
under contention or specific scheduling the read-back of mem_bytes
in restore_env compares against a stale or partially-updated entry,
returning EFAULT (22) instead of triggering the deferred
ENOSYS-panic path the test asserts.

The test then panics with errno=22 reason=`invalid_or_foreign_jump_context`
instead of errno=38 reason=`deferred_longjmp_transfer_backend`.

## Fix candidates

1. Test-only: each test should remove its registry entry on exit
   (RAII guard that calls `registry().lock().remove(&env_addr)`
   when the test scope ends).
2. Production: registry could key on (thread_id, env_addr) instead
   of just env_addr — this would handle both intentional cross-
   thread longjmp (illegal per POSIX, EINVAL is correct) and the
   stack-frame-reuse case across sequential tests on the same worker.
3. Test-only: serialize the setjmp tests with a #[serial] attribute
   or a shared TEST_LOCK Mutex so they don't race for stack address
   reuse.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P3) — test-only flake, doesn't affect production behavior.
But it does mask real failures by polluting CI noise. Suggest fix #1
(simplest) or #3 (most robust) as the swarm has time.","notes":"Fixed in 6ee22896: RegistryEntryGuard RAII wrapper added to all six setjmp_abi unit tests. Validated by 12x stress with --test-threads=16: zero longjmp_entrypoint_terminates_with_enosys_payload_in_tests failures. Three OTHER unrelated flakes surfaced in same sweep — filed separately.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:26:42.985457536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T01:35:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T01:35:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7xkx","title":"resolv: characterize safe divergence for getaddrinfo_a NULL list with positive nitems","description":"Host glibc segfaults for getaddrinfo_a with a NULL request list and positive nitems. FrankenLibC currently takes a safe fallback instead of crashing. Capture that crash boundary and document the intended safety divergence so future resolver work does not accidentally copy host UB.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:39:24.046955751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:29:21.388130149Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:29:21.385958008Z","close_reason":"Characterized the host getaddrinfo_a NULL-list crash boundary in a subprocess and documented FrankenLibC's intentional safe divergence for positive nitems with a NULL request list.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-eh10","title":"Divergent ABI source scanning between build.rs and check_replacement_guard.sh breaks standalone build","description":"`crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs` scans all `.rs` files in `src/` for `libc::` callthroughs, which catches `host_resolve.rs:51 libc::dlvsym` and breaks the `standalone` build. However, `scripts/check_replacement_guard.sh` only scans `*_abi.rs` files, missing `host_resolve.rs` entirely. Furthermore, `build.rs` does not implement the newly added `source_scan_exceptions` from `replacement_profile.json`.\n\nTo fix:\n1. Make `check_replacement_guard.sh` scan all `.rs` files (or align the scopes).\n2. Update `build.rs` to respect `source_scan_exceptions` so `host_resolve::dlvsym` (and any future exceptions) can be whitelisted.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:51:44.040354603Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T19:04:34.261001491Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T19:04:34.258633750Z","close_reason":"Current tree already aligned: build.rs, replacement guard, and standalone cargo check all agree","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ekqr","title":"Conformance: direct execute memory_ops fixture cases","description":"Add direct frankenlibc_fixture_exec coverage for memory_ops.json cases in the harness alongside existing fixture schema and coverage checks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-04-18T10:44:20.695963370Z","created_by":"SilverHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-18T11:09:06.223721340Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T11:09:06.221765368Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for memory_ops fixtures and executor support for legacy string-shaped memory_ops cases; verified with rch test/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","memory","testing"]}
+{"id":"bd-el0v8","title":"abi tests: synchronous_gai_wrappers + tmpfile + fmemopen flakes under --test-threads=16","description":"## Symptom

Three additional unit tests flake intermittently under high
parallelism (--test-threads=16, ~25% combined hit rate across 12
sweep iterations), distinct from bd-7v4oe (closed):

| Test | Hit rate (12 sweeps) | File |
|------|---------------------|------|
| `synchronous_gai_wrappers_match_host_degenerate_contracts` | 3/12 (25%) | DNS/network |
| `tmpfile_closes_without_leaking_named_tmp_entries` | 1/12 (~8%) | filesystem |
| `fmemopen_write_creates_stream` | 1/12 (~8%) | stdio |

The bd-7v4oe fix (registry RAII guard) verified its own target
flake is gone in this same sweep — these are separate races.

## Likely categories

1. **synchronous_gai_wrappers** — touches `getaddrinfo` and friends
   which read /etc/resolv.conf, /etc/hosts, etc. Under parallelism
   the host glibc resolver may serialize on shared file descriptors
   or DNS cache state, producing different EAI_* codes than
   "degenerate" baseline. Fix candidate: serialize the test (or
   subset) with a shared lock.
2. **tmpfile_closes_without_leaking_named_tmp_entries** — POSIX
   `tmpfile()` creates an unlinked file in /tmp. Under parallel
   pressure the directory enumeration check may see entries from
   other in-flight `tmpfile()` calls. Fix candidate: use a unique
   subdirectory or accept transient races in the leak count check.
3. **fmemopen_write_creates_stream** — fmemopen builds a memory-
   backed FILE*. Could share registry state with other stream
   tests. Fix candidate: same RAII registry-cleanup pattern as
   bd-7v4oe.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P3) — test-only flakes that don't affect production
behavior. Worth fixing because they pollute CI and mask real
regressions in stress sweeps. The bd-7v4oe pattern (RAII cleanup
guards) likely applies to (3); (1) and (2) need targeted analysis
of the host syscall surface they exercise.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:43:15.145493962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T01:43:15.145493962Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-emwy","title":"syscall: futex wait-bitset veneer","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T15:09:55.617591752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T15:13:44.464779692Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T15:13:44.462848217Z","close_reason":"Implemented futex wait-bitset veneer coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-epeg","title":"Cross-cutting: franken_kernel crate integration (Cx, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId)","description":"Integrate FrankenLibC with franken_kernel suite substrate: adopt canonical Cx capability context, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion types. All TSM decisions must flow through Cx. All evidence records must use canonical IDs. Must not fork franken_kernel schema. ADOPTION WEDGE: FrankenLibC must function standalone without franken_kernel (fallback to local types).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:16.769752763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:58.645348954Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T23:03:58.645135059Z","close_reason":"Delivered: code + artifacts present.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adoption-wedge","cross-crate","franken-kernel","frankenlibc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-epeg","depends_on_id":"bd-32e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-epeg","depends_on_id":"bd-oai","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1293,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Started a bounded `bd-epeg` slice by extending the local fallback ID/schema contract into `alien_cs_metrics`.

Changes:
- Updated `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/alien_cs_metrics.rs` to import the local fallback `DecisionId` and `MEMBRANE_SCHEMA_VERSION`.
- Metric event JSONL rows now emit `decision_id` and `schema_version`.
- Aggregate snapshot JSONL rows now emit `decision_id: 0` and `schema_version`.
- Tightened the Alien CS JSONL tests to require those fields and to assert deterministic values.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib`
  Expected: compile success with exit 0.
  Result: passed.
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane alien_cs_metrics -- --nocapture`
  Expected: alien_cs_metrics targeted tests pass with the updated JSONL contract.
  Result: passed, 14 passed / 0 failed.

Notes:
- `bv --robot-triage/--robot-next/--robot-insights` still ranks the TSM epic as the strongest actionable lane. This bead was the next concrete, useful step because it extends canonical fallback IDs into another structured evidence exporter without waiting for full `franken_kernel` integration.
- Agent Mail inbox remained empty and the project currently shows only `MistyGrove` registered, so there were no peer agents to notify directly.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:42:31Z"},{"id":1294,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added a second `bd-epeg` slice by aligning the HJI proof artifact schema tag with the shared fallback membrane schema.

Changes:
- Updated `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/hji_reachability.rs` to use `MEMBRANE_SCHEMA_VERSION.to_string()` instead of a one-off `"v1"` literal.
- Tightened `viability_artifact_reports_expected_kernel_counts` to assert `schema_version == "1.0"`.

Verification:

  Expected: compile success with exit 0.
  Result: passed.

  Expected: HJI artifact test passes with shared schema version.
  Result: passed, 1 passed / 0 failed.
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane proof_adversary_witnesses_are_sound -- --nocapture`
  Expected: witness proof remains valid after schema change.
  Result: passed, 1 passed / 0 failed.

Notes:
- This continues the same actionable `bd-epeg` lane chosen from `bv --robot-*`: move remaining structured/proof artifacts onto the shared fallback schema instead of isolated local version strings.
- Agent Mail remained empty and no other agents are registered in this project, so there were no direct coordination messages to send or answer.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:46:51Z"},{"id":1295,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting another bounded bd-epeg slice: aligning runtime_math/evidence JSON export with the shared fallback ID/schema contract. Reserving `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs` now and will verify with targeted rch cargo check/test once patched.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:11:34Z"},{"id":1296,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented another bounded `bd-epeg` slice in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs`.

Changes:

- Routed exported `decision_id` and `policy_id` through the canonical local wrappers (`DecisionId`, `PolicyId`) before serialization, keeping the standalone adoption wedge intact.
- Tightened `decision_card_export_is_valid_json` to require `schema_version == "1.0"` and positive exported `decision_id`/`policy_id` values.

Verification:

  Expected: compile success with exit 0.
  Result: passed (remote exit=0; artifact retrieval ended with rsync code-24 vanished-file warning after success).

 {"id":"bd-rth1","title":"bd-yos subtask: TLS handoff and teardown correctness for spawned threads","description":"Background:
- TLS handoff correctness is required for thread-local errno and future pthread_key destructor behavior.

Goal:
- Implement and verify TLS initialization/teardown handoff for spawned threads.

Deliverables:
1) TLS init protocol during thread start.
2) Per-thread errno/TLS storage linkage checks.
3) Teardown sequencing and cleanup invariants.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Thread-local values remain isolated and stable under concurrent workloads.
- TLS teardown leaves no leaked state.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for TLS setup/teardown and isolation.
- E2E concurrent tests validating cross-thread isolation.
- Structured logs with trace_id, thread_id, tls_event, key_count, cleanup_status.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:52.128037997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:33.353261867Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T06:12:33.353242521Z","close_reason":"Completed TLS handoff/teardown correctness fixes and verification evidence posted in comments.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","pthread","testing","tls"],"comments":[{"id":1142,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Codex claiming this now. Found a concrete TLS table correctness bug: removing one TID currently breaks linear-probe lookup chains for colliding TIDs, which can corrupt per-thread TLS lookup after teardown. I am implementing tombstone-based delete + collision tests now.","created_at":"2026-02-22T05:19:32Z"},{"id":1143,"issue_id":"bd-rth1","author":"Codex","text":"Completed TLS handoff/teardown hardening and verification.

Implemented:
- Fixed TLS probe-chain deletion correctness in  (tombstone semantics preserve lookup chains).
- Replaced shared fallback TSD with per-thread fallback () to prevent cross-thread leakage for unregistered threads.
- Added ABI integration tests in :
  - 
  - 
  - shared guard + native-threading force helper for deterministic setup.

-{"id": "bd-ryp2g", "title": "_IO_adjust_column / _IO_adjust_wcolumn diverge from glibc — wrong column-counter algorithm", "description": "## Defect

Both `_IO_adjust_column` (`crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/io_internal_abi.rs:2055`)
and `_IO_adjust_wcolumn` (line 2091) implement their own tab-aware
column tracker:

```rust
for i in 0..count as usize {
    let byte = ...;
    match byte {
        b'\n' | b'\r' => c = 0,
        b'\t' => c = (c + 8) & !7,
        _ => c += 1,
    }
}
```

The actual glibc implementation (sunrpc-adjacent — `libio/genops.c`) is:

```c
int _IO_adjust_column (unsigned start, const char *line, int count) {
    const char *ptr = line + count;
    while (ptr > line)
        if (*--ptr == '\n')
            return line + count - ptr - 1;
    return start + count;
}
```

The semantics is: **find the position past the last newline in the
buffer, OR add `count` to `start` if no newline.** Tabs are NOT
expanded; carriage returns are NOT zeroed; the function never iterates
forward through every byte.

This is an ABI behavioral divergence. Any consumer (e.g. older glibc-
linked C++ stream code, certain pretty-printers in third-party
libraries) that calls `_IO_adjust_column` to know "how far am I from
the last newline" gets a different answer from us than from real
glibc, especially when the buffer contains tabs or carriage returns.

Additional smaller defects in the current code:
- `c + 8` and `c += 1` arithmetic on `c: u32` panics in debug mode
  for adversarial column values near `u32::MAX` (theoretical only —
  caller would need to seed `col` near INT_MAX which is implausible).
- `c as c_int` at the end can return a negative result when `c >
  i32::MAX`.

## Fix

Rewrite both helpers to mirror glibc's "scan back from end for last
newline" algorithm. Keep the membrane policy gate intact. The wide
variant scans backward over `i32` wide-chars looking for `0x0A`.

## Severity

[HIGH] (P2). Pure correctness divergence — no memory-safety
exploit, but breaks ABI compatibility for third-party libraries that
depend on the documented glibc semantics. Pretty-printer column
counters, indent trackers, and any consumer that mixes
_IO_adjust_column results with raw text-position arithmetic will
silently produce wrong layouts.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-23T01:09:59.243184856Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-23T01:15:00.000000000Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-23T01:15:00.000000000Z", "closed_by": "cc-libc", "notes": "Fixed in fb6e5ed2: _IO_adjust_column and _IO_adjust_wcolumn rewritten to match glibc libio/genops.c — backward scan for last newline, returns either (count - newline_pos - 1) or (start + count). Tabs and CR no longer special-cased. Saturating arithmetic eliminates the debug-mode overflow panics in the old tab-expansion path. 6 regression tests pin glibc semantics. Verified 52/52 io_internal_native_file_test pass + clippy clean."}

 {"id":"bd-s170","title":"Smoke-fuzz proptests for resolver parsers","description":"Archetype 1 (Crash Detector) via proptest for 4 resolver parsers that consume arbitrary bytes: parse_hosts_line, parse_services_line, ResolverConfig::parse, encode_domain_name. Each target has: (a) never-panics on arbitrary bytes, (b) structural invariant check, (c) where applicable, biased structured-alphabet fuzz to reach deep parser paths. ~6500 case evaluations per run.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
